categories.title: THE -OLOGY FILES
categories.theme: Fields of scientific study ending in '-ology' — answer is WHAT THAT FIELD STUDIES

clues.clue: This branch of science predicts rain, shine, and everything in between by studying atmospheric conditions.
Hydrogeologists spend their careers focused on this — specifically, water found beneath the Earth's surface.
Gerontologists devote their research to this stage of the human lifespan, studying its diseases and social challenges.
Asteroseismologists listen to subtle vibrations passing through distant stellar bodies in order to learn about this.
The rare discipline of Oneirology takes place largely while its researchers are unconscious, studying this nocturnal mental phenomenon.

categories.title: BODY OF KNOWLEDGE
categories.theme: Facts about the human body and its systems — answer is the BODY PART, SUBSTANCE, or MEDICAL TERM

clues.clue: This sac-like organ in the lower abdomen holds liquid waste until you visit the restroom.
These paired bean-shaped organs filter toxins and waste products from the bloodstream, producing urine.
Network of glands including the thyroid and adrenal glands, these are collectively responsible for secreting hormones into the bloodstream.
This tree-like extension on a nerve cell receives incoming chemical signals from neighboring neurons at its many branches.
Weighing roughly 1.5 kilograms in a typical adult, this organ uses about 20% of the body's total energy despite being only 2% of its mass.

finals.title: LAWS OF NATURE
finals.clue: Named for a 17th-century Irish scientist, this chemistry law states that at constant temperature, the pressure and volume of a gas are inversely proportional — double the pressure, halve the volume.

finals.title: ASTRONOMICAL ODDITIES
finals.clue: Unlike every other planet, this innermost world of our solar system can never be seen against a fully dark sky — it always sets before true darkness falls or rises with the dawn.
